title: herniaria vulgaris subsp. hirsuta
intro: Herniaria vulgaris subsp. hirsuta, also known as the hairy rampion or hirsute rampion, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
